expectations.text: Creates NamedFetcher instance with correct baseURL
expectations.evidence: Code shows: new NamedFetcher('api', { baseURL: 'https://api.example.com', ... })
Code shows: timeout: 5000

expectations.text: Adds request interceptor for auth token
expectations.evidence: Code shows: apiFetcher.interceptors.request.use({ name: 'auth-request-interceptor', order: 100, intercept(exchange) { const token = localStorage.getItem('authToken'); if (token) { exchange.request.headers.Authorization = 'Bearer ${token}'; } return exchange; } })
Code shows: const response = await fetcher.get('/users/{id}', { urlParams: { path: { id: 123 }, query: { include: 'profile' } } })
Code shows: '/users/{id}' with urlParams.path: { id: 123 }
Code shows: urlParams: { path: { id: 123 }, query: { include: 'profile' } }
Code shows: urlParams: { path: { id: 123 }, query: { include: 'profile' } }
Code shows: const user = await response.json<User>()
Code shows: new NamedFetcher('auth', { baseURL: 'https://auth.example.com', timeout: 5000 })
Code shows: new NamedFetcher('api', { baseURL: 'https://api.example.com/v1', timeout: 10000 })
Code shows: new NamedFetcher('files', { baseURL: 'https://files.example.com', timeout: 60000 })

Code shows: const authFetcher = fetcherRegistrar.requiredGet('auth'); const apiFetcher = fetcherRegistrar.requiredGet('api'); const filesFetcher = fetcherRegistrar.requiredGet('files'); Also uses fetcherRegistrar.get('api') for safe retrieval

expectations.text: Creates NamedFetcher instance with correct baseURL
expectations.evidence: Code uses Fetcher class directly (new Fetcher(...)) instead of NamedFetcher. Does not use @ahoo-wang/fetcher NamedFetcher.

No NamedFetcher is created. Uses createFetcher() function returning a Fetcher instance, but timeout is not set (TIMEOUT_MS=5000 is defined but only passed via comment, not actually used in the interceptor or Fetcher constructor options).

expectations.text: Adds request interceptor for auth token
expectations.evidence: Code uses bearerTokenInterceptor with Fetcher.interceptors.request.use(), but this is not NamedFetcher. The interceptor itself is correctly implemented, but the pattern is wrong for NamedFetcher.
Code shows: const response = await fetcher.get<User>('/users/{id}', { urlParams: { path: { id: 123 }, query: { include: 'profile' } } })
Code shows: '/users/{id}' with urlParams.path: { id: 123 }
Code shows: urlParams: { path: { id: 123 }, query: { include: 'profile' } }
Code shows: urlParams: { path: { id: 123 }, query: { include: 'profile' } }
Code shows: const user: User = await response.json<User>()
Code shows: const authFetcher = new NamedFetcher('auth', { baseURL: 'https://auth.example.com', timeout: 10000 })
Code shows: const apiFetcher = new NamedFetcher('api', { baseURL: 'https://api.example.com', timeout: 30000 })
Code shows: const filesFetcher = new NamedFetcher('files', { baseURL: 'https://files.example.com', timeout: 120000 })

Code shows: const retrievedAuthFetcher = fetcherRegistrar.get('auth'); const retrievedApiFetcher = fetcherRegistrar.get('api'); const retrievedFilesFetcher = fetcherRegistrar.get('files'); Also uses fetcherRegistrar.requiredGet('auth')
